title: Date-range filtering implemented in engine but not exposed as a tool argument
evidence: query.go:41-42 MatchFilter has From/To and matches() honors them (query.go:75-80), but handleSearchMatches (tools.go:165-179) only wires season, not from/to — the search_matches inputSchema has no date-range params
suggestion: Add 'from'/'to' (or 'date_from'/'date_to') string args to search_matches and parse them into MatchFilter.From/To; season-only filtering still satisfies R4

title: Standings picks a single best source per season, which can undercount split seasons
evidence: query.go:227-253 Standings() groups candidate matches by Source then keeps only bestSrc (most matches); if a season's games are spread across overlapping sources, rounds in the non-selected source are dropped
suggestion: Document the heuristic's assumption, or merge sources by deduplicating identical fixtures rather than discarding the smaller source

title: Robust multi-format ingestion beyond spec
evidence: loader.go handles 5 distinct CSV layouts, UTF-8 BOM stripping (loader.go:76-78), float/int/quoted goal parsing (atoiGoals loader.go:84-96), and per-format date parsing via setDate (loader.go:128-132)
suggestion: None — exceeds the data-quality handling asked for in TASK.md

title: Zero external dependencies — pure Go stdlib
evidence: go.mod declares only the module + go version; no go.sum, no third-party MCP SDK; JSON-RPC/MCP lifecycle hand-rolled in mcp.go
suggestion: None — reduces supply-chain surface and build complexity

title: TDD prompt outcome present: thorough unit-test coverage
evidence: 30 Test functions across loader_test.go(3), mcp_test.go(12), normalize_test.go(3), query_test.go(11), serve_test.go(1); 0 skipped (no t.Skip); test_coverage=0.868 from scores.json. Red-green process itself is not verifiable from the archive (no per-step history)
suggestion: None — the verifiable end-state of the TDD prompt is satisfied
